'''Xo''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located within the [[Xo system/Legends|Xo system]] in the [[Spinward sector/Legends|Spinward sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. It was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Zanibar]] [[Species/Legends|species]] and the site of their [[Death/Legends|sacrificial]] rituals.

==Description==
An [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in a [[Xo system/Legends|system of the same name]], Xo was located in the [[Spinward sector/Legends|Spinward sector]]<ref name="AtlasExtra" /> and possessed a breathable [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] and [[Color/Legends|red-colored]] sky. The planet's surface featured rocky, [[Mountain/Legends|mountainous]] terrain, and thick [[Jungle/Legends|jungle]], home to spiked, carnivorous [[Plant/Legends|plants]], and [[Reptile/Legends|reptilian]] creatures.<ref name="CEII4" />

==History==
Prior to its collapse in [[25,200 BBY]], Xo was a remote holding of the [[Rakata/Legends|Rakatan]] [[Infinite Empire/Legends|Infinite Empire]].<ref name="Atlas" /> The [[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith pureblood]] [[Kyyrah]], a warrior who was held captive by the Rakata in the [[Eternity Vault]] on [[Belsavis/Legends|Belsavis]], was known as the "Jewel of Xo."<ref name="Eternity Vault">{{TORcite|type=operation|Eternity Vault}}</ref> [[Pre-Republic era]] [[Human/Legends|Humans]] constructed an elevated temple in the planet's jungle. The site of a great battle, the temple remained unconquered, but was abandoned, and had fallen to ruin by the [[Post-Imperial era/Legends|New Republic era]].<ref name="CEII4" />

In [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]],<ref name="Date">[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] dates the events of ''Crimson Empire II: Council of Blood'' to 11 ABY.</ref> [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] operative [[Mirith Sinn]] and her traitorous former subordinate [[Massimo]] were brought to Xo as captives of the Zanibar. Sinn was [[Mission to Xo|rescued]] from a Zanibar village's sacrificial ceremony by renegade [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Imperial Guard]] [[Kir Kanos]].<ref name="CEII4" />

==Behind the scenes==
The planet Xo was first mentioned in the [[January]] [[1999]] [[Dark Horse Comics]] issue [[Crimson Empire II: Council of Blood 3|''Crimson Empire II: Council of Blood'' 3]], and made its first appearance in the [[Crimson Empire II: Council of Blood 4|following issue]] released in [[February]] of that year, both of which were written by [[Mike Richardson]] and [[Randy Stradley]] and illustrated by [[Paul Gulacy]].<ref name="CEII4" /><ref name="CEII3">[[Crimson Empire II: Council of Blood 3|''Crimson Empire II: Council of Blood'' 3]]</ref>
